    ANALYSIS FRAMEWORK - Provide detailed analysis for each section:

    ## BUSINESS STRATEGY ANALYSIS
    Evaluate the company's competitive position and sustainability:
    - Industry dynamics and competitive landscape
    - Sustainable competitive advantages (moats)
    - Business model strengths and vulnerabilities
    - Key value drivers and profit engines
    - Strategic positioning relative to peers

    ## ACCOUNTING ANALYSIS
    Assess the quality and reliability of financial reporting:
    - Earnings quality assessment (accruals, cash conversion, sustainability)
    - Key accounting policies and their impact on reported results
    - Identification of any red flags or aggressive accounting practices
    - Conservative vs. aggressive accounting choices
    - Off-balance sheet items and hidden liabilities
    - Revenue recognition quality and sustainability

    ## FINANCIAL ANALYSIS
    Conduct comprehensive ratio analysis using the data provided:
    
    ### Profitability Analysis:
    - ROE decomposition (ROE = ROA × Equity Multiplier)
    - ROA breakdown (Net Margin × Asset Turnover)
    - Trend analysis of margins (gross, operating, net)
    - Profitability sustainability and drivers
    - Comparison with industry benchmarks

    ### Liquidity Assessment:
    - Current ratio, quick ratio, cash ratio analysis
    - Operating working capital management
    - Cash conversion cycle efficiency
    - Short-term liquidity stress testing

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios
    - Debt service capability
    - Financial leverage impact on ROE
    - Long-term financial stability

    ### Efficiency Metrics:
    - Asset turnover ratios (total, fixed, working capital)
    - Inventory and receivables management
    - Operating efficiency indicators
    - Management effectiveness in asset utilization

    ## PROSPECTIVE ANALYSIS
    Forward-looking assessment and valuation:
    - Growth sustainability analysis
    - Future earnings power assessment
    - Key assumptions for projections
    - Scenario analysis (bull, base, bear cases)
    - Valuation using appropriate multiples
    - Risk factors and sensitivity analysis

    ## INVESTMENT RECOMMENDATION
    Synthesize analysis into clear investment decision:
    - Overall investment thesis (2-3 sentences)
    - Recommended action (BUY/HOLD/SELL) with rationale
    - Target price range based on analysis
    - Key catalysts and risks
    - Time horizon and investor suitability
    - Confidence level in recommendation

    Business Description: PETRONAS Dagangan Berhad is a Malaysia-based retailer and marketer of liquefied petroleum gas. The Company operates through three segments: Retail, Commercial, and Convenience. The Retail segment consists of the sales and purchasing of petroleum products to the retail sector. The Commercial segment consists of sales and purchase of petroleum products and provision of services to the commercial sector. The Convenience segment comprises mainly non-fuel business activities. Its products include aviation fuels, diesel, fuel oil, petroleum coke, bitumen, sulphur, mogas, kerosene, methanol, and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG). Its range of fuel products includes PETRONAS Primax 97 with Pro-Race, PETRONAS Primax 95 with Pro-Drive, PETRONAS Dynamic Diesel Euro 5 with Pro-Drive (B7), PETRONAS Dynamic Diesel Euro 5 with Pro-Drive (B10), and PETRONAS Dynamic Diesel Euro 5 with Pro-Drive (B20).     equity_multiplier = df_balance.loc[year, 'total_assets'] / df_balance.loc[year, 'total_equity']

    print(f"{year}:")

Its soft line includes bedding, home textiles, furniture, lighting, household decorative products, and household accessories.     Business Description: Indorama Ventures Public Company Limited is a Thailand-based petrochemicals producer. It operates through three segments: Combined PET, Indovinya and Fibers. The Combined PET segment manufactures and distributes the full polyethylene terephthalate (PET) value chain comprising Paraxylene (PX), purified terephthalic acid (PTA), PET, and Recycling (Integrated PET), Specialty PET-related chemicals, PIA, and NDC (Specialty Chemicals), PET preforms and packaging (Packaging), and Ethane to MEG and MTBE (Intermediates Chemicals). The Indovinya segment manufactures and distributes Integrated Downstream Surfactants. The Fibers segment is engaged in the manufacturing and distribution of Polyester, Rayon, Nylon, Polypropylene, composites, and worsted wool fibers, for three end-use segments: mobility, lifestyle, and hygiene. Its subsidiaries include Indorama Ventures Mobility (Qingdao) Co., Ltd, Indorama Ventures Lifestyle (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., Sinterama Yarns (Dongguan) Co., Ltd, among others.

    ## BUSINESS STRATEGY ANALYSIS
    Evaluate the company's competitive position and sustainability:
    - Industry dynamics and competitive landscape
    - Sustainable competitive advantages (moats)
    - Business model strengths and vulnerabilities
    - Key value drivers and profit engines
    - Strategic positioning relative to peers

    ## ACCOUNTING ANALYSIS
    Assess the quality and reliability of financial reporting:
    - Earnings quality assessment (accruals, cash conversion, sustainability)
    - Key accounting policies and their impact on reported results
    - Identification of any red flags or aggressive accounting practices
    - Conservative vs. aggressive accounting choices
    - Off-balance sheet items and hidden liabilities
    - Revenue recognition quality and sustainability

    ## FINANCIAL ANALYSIS
    Conduct comprehensive ratio analysis using the data provided:
    
    ### Profitability Analysis:
    - ROE decomposition (ROE = ROA × Equity Multiplier)
    - ROA breakdown (Net Margin × Asset Turnover)
    - Trend analysis of margins (gross, operating, net)
    - Profitability sustainability and drivers
    - Comparison with industry benchmarks

    ### Liquidity Assessment:
    - Current ratio, quick ratio, cash ratio analysis
    - Operating working capital management
    - Cash conversion cycle efficiency
    - Short-term liquidity stress testing

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios
    - Debt service capability
    - Financial leverage impact on ROE
    - Long-term financial stability

    ### Efficiency Metrics:
    - Asset turnover ratios (total, fixed, working capital)
    - Inventory and receivables management
    - Operating efficiency indicators
    - Management effectiveness in asset utilization

    ## PROSPECTIVE ANALYSIS
    Forward-looking assessment and valuation:
    - Growth sustainability analysis
    - Future earnings power assessment
    - Key assumptions for projections
    - Scenario analysis (bull, base, bear cases)
    - Valuation using appropriate multiples
    - Risk factors and sensitivity analysis

    ## FINANCIAL ANALYSIS

    - ROE decomposition (ROE = ROA × Equity Multiplier)

    ### Liquidity Assessment:

    - Cash conversion cycle efficiency
    - Short-term liquidity stress testing

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios
    - Debt service capability
    - Financial leverage impact on ROE
    - Long-term financial stability

    ### Efficiency Metrics:
    - Asset turnover ratios (total, fixed, working capital)

    ## PROSPECTIVE ANALYSIS

    ## BUSINESS STRATEGY ANALYSIS

    ## FINANCIAL ANALYSIS
    Conduct comprehensive ratio analysis using the data provided:
    
    ### Profitability Analysis:
    - ROE decomposition (ROE = ROA × Equity Multiplier)
    - ROA breakdown (Net Margin × Asset Turnover)
    - Trend analysis of margins (gross, operating, net)
    - Profitability sustainability and drivers
    - Comparison with industry benchmarks

    ### Liquidity Assessment:
    - Current ratio, quick ratio, cash ratio analysis
    - Operating working capital management
    - Cash conversion cycle efficiency
    - Short-term liquidity stress testing

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios
    - Debt service capability
    - Financial leverage impact on ROE
    - Long-term financial stability

    ### Efficiency Metrics:
    - Asset turnover ratios (total, fixed, working capital)
    - Inventory and receivables management
    - Operating efficiency indicators
    - Management effectiveness in asset utilization

    ## PROSPECTIVE ANALYSIS

    - Growth sustainability analysis
    - Future earnings power assessment
    - Key assumptions for projections

    - Valuation using appropriate multiples
    - Risk factors and sensitivity analysis

    ## BUSINESS STRATEGY ANALYSIS

    ## FINANCIAL ANALYSIS
    Conduct comprehensive ratio analysis using the data provided:
    
    ### Profitability Analysis:
    - ROE decomposition (ROE = ROA × Equity Multiplier)
    - ROA breakdown (Net Margin × Asset Turnover)
    - Trend analysis of margins (gross, operating, net)
    - Profitability sustainability and drivers
    - Comparison with industry benchmarks

    ### Liquidity Assessment:
    - Current ratio, quick ratio, cash ratio analysis
    - Operating working capital management
    - Cash conversion cycle efficiency
    - Short-term liquidity stress testing

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios
    - Debt service capability
    - Financial leverage impact on ROE
    - Long-term financial stability

    ### Efficiency Metrics:
    - Asset turnover ratios (total, fixed, working capital)
    - Inventory and receivables management
    - Operating efficiency indicators
    - Management effectiveness in asset utilization

    ## PROSPECTIVE ANALYSIS

    - Growth sustainability analysis

    - Valuation using appropriate multiples

It operates in four segments. The Data Center segment includes artificial intelligence (AI) accelerators, server central processing units (CPUs), graphics processing units (GPUs), accelerated processing units (APUs), data processing units (DPUs), field programmable gate arrays (FPGAs), smart network interface cards (SmartNICs), and Adaptive system-on-chip (SoC) products for data centers. The Client segment primarily includes CPUs, APUs, and chipsets for desktops and notebooks. The Gaming segment primarily includes discrete GPUs, semi-custom SoC products and development services. The Embedded segment includes embedded CPUs, GPUs, APUs, FPGAs, system on modules (SOMs), and Adaptive SoC products. It markets and sells its products under the AMD trademark. Its products include AMD EPYC, AMD Ryzen, AMD Ryzen PRO, Virtex UltraScale+ and others.

    ### Liquidity Assessment:

    - Cash conversion cycle efficiency
    - Short-term liquidity stress testing

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios
    - Debt service capability
    - Financial leverage impact on ROE
    - Long-term financial stability

    ### Efficiency Metrics:
    - Asset turnover ratios (total, fixed, working capital)

    ## BUSINESS STRATEGY ANALYSIS

    - Off-balance sheet items and hidden liabilities

    ## FINANCIAL ANALYSIS
    Conduct comprehensive ratio analysis using the data provided:
    
    ### Profitability Analysis:
    - ROE decomposition (ROE = ROA × Equity Multiplier)
    - ROA breakdown (Net Margin × Asset Turnover)
    - Trend analysis of margins (gross, operating, net)
    - Profitability sustainability and drivers
    - Comparison with industry benchmarks

    ### Liquidity Assessment:
    - Current ratio, quick ratio, cash ratio analysis
    - Operating working capital management
    - Cash conversion cycle efficiency
    - Short-term liquidity stress testing

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios
    - Debt service capability
    - Financial leverage impact on ROE
    - Long-term financial stability

    ### Efficiency Metrics:
    - Asset turnover ratios (total, fixed, working capital)
    - Inventory and receivables management
    - Operating efficiency indicators
    - Management effectiveness in asset utilization

    ## PROSPECTIVE ANALYSIS
    Forward-looking assessment and valuation:
    - Growth sustainability analysis
    - Future earnings power assessment
    - Key assumptions for projections
    - Scenario analysis (bull, base, bear cases)
    - Valuation using appropriate multiples
    - Risk factors and sensitivity analysis

    Business Description: Eli Lilly and Company is a medicine company, which discovers, develops, manufactures and markets products in the human pharmaceutical products segment. Its cardiometabolic health products include Basaglar; Humalog, Humalog Mix 75/25, Humalog U-100, Humalog U-200, Humalog Mix 50/50, insulin lispro, and others; Humulin, Humulin 70/30, and others; Jardiance; Mounjaro; Trulicity; and Zepbound. Its oncology products include Cyramza, Erbitux, Tyvyt, and Verzenio. Its immunology products include Ebglyss, Olumiant, Omvoh, and Taltz. Its neuroscience products include Emgality and Kisunla. The Company, through its subsidiary, POINT Biopharma Global Inc., is engaged in radiopharmaceutical discovery, development, and manufacturing efforts, as well as clinical and pre-clinical radioligand therapies in development for the treatment of cancer. It is also developing an oral small molecule inhibitor of α4β7 integrin for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). It also owns a lead therapeutic molecule FXR314.

    ## BUSINESS STRATEGY ANALYSIS

    - Industry dynamics and competitive landscape

    ## ACCOUNTING ANALYSIS
    Assess the quality and reliability of financial reporting:
    - Earnings quality assessment (accruals, cash conversion, sustainability)
    - Key accounting policies and their impact on reported results

    - Conservative vs. aggressive accounting choices
    - Off-balance sheet items and hidden liabilities
    - Revenue recognition quality and sustainability

    ## FINANCIAL ANALYSIS
    Conduct comprehensive ratio analysis using the data provided:
    
    ### Profitability Analysis:
    - ROE decomposition (ROE = ROA × Equity Multiplier)
    - ROA breakdown (Net Margin × Asset Turnover)
    - Trend analysis of margins (gross, operating, net)
    - Profitability sustainability and drivers
    - Comparison with industry benchmarks

    ### Liquidity Assessment:
    - Current ratio, quick ratio, cash ratio analysis
    - Operating working capital management
    - Cash conversion cycle efficiency
    - Short-term liquidity stress testing

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios
    - Debt service capability
    - Financial leverage impact on ROE
    - Long-term financial stability

    ### Efficiency Metrics:
    - Asset turnover ratios (total, fixed, working capital)
    - Inventory and receivables management
    - Operating efficiency indicators
    - Management effectiveness in asset utilization

    ## PROSPECTIVE ANALYSIS
    Forward-looking assessment and valuation:
    - Growth sustainability analysis
    - Future earnings power assessment
    - Key assumptions for projections
    - Scenario analysis (bull, base, bear cases)
    - Valuation using appropriate multiples
    - Risk factors and sensitivity analysis

    Business Description: Chememan Public Company Limited is a Thailand-based company engaged in the production and distribution of quicklime, hydrated lime, limestone, and ground limestone. Its geographic segments include Thailand and overseas. Its products include Quicklime, or Calcium Oxide (CaO); Hydrated Lime, or Calcium Hydroxide (Ca(OH)2); and Limestone and Ground Limestone, or Calcium Carbonate (CaCO3), marketed under the trademark CHEMEMAN. These products are used in various applications such as ferrous and non-ferrous metal mining, pulp and paper, sugar production, bioplastics, iron and steel, chemicals and petrochemicals, agriculture, flue gas treatment and water purification, glass and bottle manufacturing, and specialty fiberglass. It also provides services including Technical Support, Transportation, Material Handling, Quality Assurance (QA), and Research and Development (R&D). Its subsidiaries include Northman Company Limited, Chememan Australia Pty Ltd, and Chememan India Private Limited.

    Business Description: Occidental Petroleum Corporation is an international energy company with assets primarily in the United States, the Middle East and North Africa. The Company is an oil and gas producer in the United States, including a producer in the Permian and DJ basins, and the offshore Gulf of Mexico. It operates through three segments: oil and gas, chemical and midstream and marketing. The oil and gas segment explores for, develops, and produces oil (which includes condensate), natural gas liquids (NGL) and natural gas. The chemical segment primarily manufactures and markets basic chemicals and vinyls. The midstream and marketing segment purchases, markets, gathers, processes, transports, and stores oil (which includes condensate), NGL, natural gas, carbon dioxide (CO2) and power. The midstream and marketing segment provides flow assurance and maximizes the value of its oil and gas. It also optimizes its transportation and storage capacity and invests in entities that conduct similar activities.

The Company operates a fleet of over 329 aircrafts. Its passenger aircrafts include Airbus A319-100, Airbus A350-900, Boeing 767-300ER, Boeing 787-8, Boeing 787-9 and Boeing 777-300ER.

    ## FINANCIAL ANALYSIS

    ### Profitability Analysis:
    - ROE decomposition (ROE = ROA × Equity Multiplier)
    - ROA breakdown (Net Margin × Asset Turnover)

    ### Liquidity Assessment:

    - Cash conversion cycle efficiency
    - Short-term liquidity stress testing

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios
    - Debt service capability
    - Financial leverage impact on ROE
    - Long-term financial stability

    ### Efficiency Metrics:
    - Asset turnover ratios (total, fixed, working capital)
    - Inventory and receivables management
    - Operating efficiency indicators
    - Management effectiveness in asset utilization

    ## PROSPECTIVE ANALYSIS

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends

    ### Efficiency Metrics:

It operates in four segments. The Data Center segment includes artificial intelligence (AI) accelerators, server central processing units (CPUs), graphics processing units (GPUs), accelerated processing units (APUs), data processing units (DPUs), field programmable gate arrays (FPGAs), smart network interface cards (SmartNICs), and Adaptive system-on-chip (SoC) products for data centers. The Client segment primarily includes CPUs, APUs, and chipsets for desktops and notebooks. The Gaming segment primarily includes discrete GPUs, semi-custom SoC products and development services. The Embedded segment includes embedded CPUs, GPUs, APUs, FPGAs, system on modules (SOMs), and Adaptive SoC products. It markets and sells its products under the AMD trademark.     Business Description: Tiong Nam Logistics Holdings Berhad is a Malaysia-based investment holding company. The Company is engaged in the trading of diesel and petrol. It operates through four segments: Logistics & Warehousing Services, Investments, Property Development Projects, and Dormitory. It provides fully integrated logistics services, which includes provision of warehouse space coupled with warehouse management services, trucking delivery, cross-border transfer, container haulage, heavy transportation, as well as last-mile delivery for e-commerce companies. Its services include total logistics solutions and specific solutions. Its portfolio includes the development of commercial, industrial, and residential properties. With a regional network of 79 facilities in seven countries, it offers comprehensive logistics solutions, be it domestic trucking or a fully integrated 3PL solution. Its specific solutions include cold chain services, courier services, heavy transport (project cargo) and others.

The Company is focused on the business of manufacturing and sale of passenger and commercial vehicles. The Company’s vehicles are offered through three channels: NEXA, Arena, and Commercial. It also sells aftermarket parts and accessories under the brand name of Maruti Suzuki Genuine Parts, and Maruti Suzuki Genuine Accessories. The other activities of the Company comprise facilitation of pre-owned car sales, fleet management. and car financing. Its portfolio of Nexa products includes XL6, Ciaz, The New Age Baleno, Ignis, Grand Vitara, FRONX, Jimny, and Invicto. Its portfolio of Arena products includes Alto K10, Brezza, Celerio, WagonR, Swift, Dzire, S-Presso, Eeco, Dezire, and Ertiga. Its portfolio of Commercial products includes Super Carry and Eeco Cargo.     ANALYSIS FRAMEWORK - Provide detailed analysis for each section:

    ## BUSINESS STRATEGY ANALYSIS
    Evaluate the company's competitive position and sustainability:
    - Industry dynamics and competitive landscape
    - Sustainable competitive advantages (moats)
    - Business model strengths and vulnerabilities
    - Key value drivers and profit engines
    - Strategic positioning relative to peers

    ## ACCOUNTING ANALYSIS
    Assess the quality and reliability of financial reporting:
    - Earnings quality assessment (accruals, cash conversion, sustainability)
    - Key accounting policies and their impact on reported results
    - Identification of any red flags or aggressive accounting practices
    - Conservative vs. aggressive accounting choices
    - Off-balance sheet items and hidden liabilities
    - Revenue recognition quality and sustainability

    ## FINANCIAL ANALYSIS
    Conduct comprehensive ratio analysis using the data provided:
    
    ### Profitability Analysis:
    - ROE decomposition (ROE = ROA × Equity Multiplier)
    - ROA breakdown (Net Margin × Asset Turnover)
    - Trend analysis of margins (gross, operating, net)
    - Profitability sustainability and drivers
    - Comparison with industry benchmarks

    ### Liquidity Assessment:
    - Current ratio, quick ratio, cash ratio analysis
    - Operating working capital management
    - Cash conversion cycle efficiency
    - Short-term liquidity stress testing

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios
    - Debt service capability
    - Financial leverage impact on ROE
    - Long-term financial stability

    ### Efficiency Metrics:
    - Asset turnover ratios (total, fixed, working capital)
    - Inventory and receivables management
    - Operating efficiency indicators
    - Management effectiveness in asset utilization

    ## PROSPECTIVE ANALYSIS
    Forward-looking assessment and valuation:
    - Growth sustainability analysis
    - Future earnings power assessment
    - Key assumptions for projections
    - Scenario analysis (bull, base, bear cases)
    - Valuation using appropriate multiples
    - Risk factors and sensitivity analysis

    ## INVESTMENT RECOMMENDATION

    - Target price range based on analysis

    - Confidence level in recommendation

    ## FINANCIAL ANALYSIS
    Conduct comprehensive ratio analysis using the data provided:
    
    ### Profitability Analysis:
    - ROE decomposition (ROE = ROA × Equity Multiplier)
    - ROA breakdown (Net Margin × Asset Turnover)
    - Trend analysis of margins (gross, operating, net)
    - Profitability sustainability and drivers

    ### Liquidity Assessment:
    - Current ratio, quick ratio, cash ratio analysis
    - Operating working capital management
    - Cash conversion cycle efficiency
    - Short-term liquidity stress testing

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios
    - Debt service capability
    - Financial leverage impact on ROE
    - Long-term financial stability

    ### Efficiency Metrics:
    - Asset turnover ratios (total, fixed, working capital)
    - Inventory and receivables management
    - Operating efficiency indicators
    - Management effectiveness in asset utilization

    ## PROSPECTIVE ANALYSIS

    - Growth sustainability analysis

    - Key assumptions for projections

    - Valuation using appropriate multiples

    ## BUSINESS STRATEGY ANALYSIS

    ## FINANCIAL ANALYSIS
    Conduct comprehensive ratio analysis using the data provided:
    
    ### Profitability Analysis:
    - ROE decomposition (ROE = ROA × Equity Multiplier)
    - ROA breakdown (Net Margin × Asset Turnover)
    - Trend analysis of margins (gross, operating, net)
    - Profitability sustainability and drivers
    - Comparison with industry benchmarks

    ### Liquidity Assessment:
    - Current ratio, quick ratio, cash ratio analysis
    - Operating working capital management
    - Cash conversion cycle efficiency
    - Short-term liquidity stress testing

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios
    - Debt service capability
    - Financial leverage impact on ROE
    - Long-term financial stability

    ### Efficiency Metrics:
    - Asset turnover ratios (total, fixed, working capital)
    - Inventory and receivables management
    - Operating efficiency indicators
    - Management effectiveness in asset utilization

    ## PROSPECTIVE ANALYSIS

    - Growth sustainability analysis

    - Valuation using appropriate multiples

    ## BUSINESS STRATEGY ANALYSIS
    Evaluate the company's competitive position and sustainability:
    - Industry dynamics and competitive landscape
    - Sustainable competitive advantages (moats)
    - Business model strengths and vulnerabilities
    - Key value drivers and profit engines
    - Strategic positioning relative to peers

    ## ACCOUNTING ANALYSIS
    Assess the quality and reliability of financial reporting:
    - Earnings quality assessment (accruals, cash conversion, sustainability)
    - Key accounting policies and their impact on reported results
    - Identification of any red flags or aggressive accounting practices
    - Conservative vs. aggressive accounting choices
    - Off-balance sheet items and hidden liabilities
    - Revenue recognition quality and sustainability

    ## FINANCIAL ANALYSIS
    Conduct comprehensive ratio analysis using the data provided:
    
    ### Profitability Analysis:
    - ROE decomposition (ROE = ROA × Equity Multiplier)
    - ROA breakdown (Net Margin × Asset Turnover)
    - Trend analysis of margins (gross, operating, net)
    - Profitability sustainability and drivers
    - Comparison with industry benchmarks

    ### Liquidity Assessment:
    - Current ratio, quick ratio, cash ratio analysis
    - Operating working capital management
    - Cash conversion cycle efficiency
    - Short-term liquidity stress testing

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios
    - Debt service capability
    - Financial leverage impact on ROE
    - Long-term financial stability

    ### Efficiency Metrics:
    - Asset turnover ratios (total, fixed, working capital)
    - Inventory and receivables management
    - Operating efficiency indicators
    - Management effectiveness in asset utilization

    ## PROSPECTIVE ANALYSIS
    Forward-looking assessment and valuation:
    - Growth sustainability analysis
    - Future earnings power assessment
    - Key assumptions for projections
    - Scenario analysis (bull, base, bear cases)
    - Valuation using appropriate multiples
    - Risk factors and sensitivity analysis

    Business Description: General Dynamics Corporation is a global aerospace and defense company. It offers a portfolio of products and services in business aviation; ship construction and repair; land combat vehicles, weapons systems and munitions, and technology products and services. Its segments include Aerospace, Marine Systems, Combat Systems and Technologies. The Aerospace segment produces business jets and is the standard bearer in new technology aircraft, aircraft repair, customer support and custom completion services. The Marine Systems segment designs and builds nuclear-powered submarines and is engaged in surface combatant and auxiliary ship design and construction for the U.S. Navy.     ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios

    ### Efficiency Metrics:

    Business Description: Bristol-Myers Squibb Company is a global biopharmaceutical company. It is engaged in the discovery, development and delivery of transformational medicines for patients facing serious diseases in areas: oncology, hematology, immunology, cardiovascular, neuroscience and other areas. Its platforms comprise chemically synthesized or small molecule drugs, including protein degraders; drugs produced from biological processes, called biologics; ADCs, CAR-T cell therapies, and radiopharmaceutical therapeutics. Small molecule drugs are typically administered orally in the form of a tablet or capsule, although other drug delivery mechanisms are also used. Biologics are typically administered through injections or by intravenous infusion. CAR-T cell therapies are administered by intravenous infusion. Its growth portfolio includes Opdivo, Opdivo Qvantig, Orencia, Yervoy, Reblozyl, Opdualag, and others. Its legacy portfolio includes Eliquis, Revlimid, Pomalyst/Imnovid, Sprycel and Abraxane.

    ### Liquidity Assessment:

    - Short-term liquidity stress testing

    ### Solvency Analysis:
    - Debt-to-equity ratios and trends
    - Interest coverage ratios
    - Debt service capability
    - Financial leverage impact on ROE
    - Long-term financial stability

    ### Efficiency Metrics:
    - Asset turnover ratios (total, fixed, working capital)

    Business Description: PTT Oil and Retail Business Public Company Limited is a Thailand-based company engaged in sales and distribution of petroleum products, retail business and other services (non-oil), and investing in related companies. The Company invests in related companies that are engaged in the management of fuel stations, convenience stores, space management in fuel stations, personnel service to related parties, blending and bottling of lube oil, and other fuel-related businesses. The Company operates through three segments: Mobility Business, Lifestyle Business, and Global Business. The Mobility Business is engaged in the marketing of petroleum products through the retail and commercial business of the Company and its subsidiaries in Thailand. Lifestyle Business is engaged in the Cafe Amazon business, convenience stores, food and beverage retail stores of the Company and its subsidiaries in Thailand. Global Business includes the oil business, retail business and other services.
